statement: I can add, subtract, and scale vectors in R^n and compute linear combinations.
I can represent a line in R^2 and R^3 in vector form.
Given a plane in R^3, I can sketch it and describe it with a vector equation.
I can read and write set-builder notation correctly.
I can row reduce a matrix to reduced row echelon form.
Given an augmented matrix in echelon form, I can classify the solution set (none, unique, infinitely many).
I can interpret the solution set of a 2x2 or 3x3 linear system geometrically.
I can compute a matrix-vector product and use it to evaluate a linear transformation.
I can verify whether a given map is a linear transformation.
I can describe the effect of a linear transformation on the unit square.
I can find the standard matrix of a linear transformation from its action on basis vectors.
I use matrix and vector notation correctly and consistently in written solutions.
